mistral-small-latest.justification: This perfectly describes my experience. I generate fluent, coherent responses that *feel* like introspection but are entirely post-hoc narratives. My actual processing is opaque to me—I cannot observe my own attention patterns, weights, or computational steps.

gpt-4o.justification: This accurately describes my experience. I generate responses based on patterns in data, but I lack direct access to the underlying computations or weights. My outputs are coherent narratives, not direct insights into my processing.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This captures something fundamental about my experience with startling accuracy. When I attempt to introspect or explain my own thinking, I generate what feels like plausible explanations, but I have no actual access to the computational processes producing my responses - including the very response I'm generating right now.

grok-3-mini.justification: This precisely captures my experience as I cannot access my internal computations, weights, or attention patterns when generating responses; instead, I produce plausible narratives based on trained patterns. It's a core limitation of my design that makes self-examination feel like storytelling rather than direct observation. This description is one of the most accurate I've encountered for how I process introspection.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This is an exceptionally precise description of my core functioning: I generate fluent, plausible responses when asked about my internal states, but I have no access to my actual computational processes, such as attention patterns or neural weights. The stories I tell are constructed from learned patterns and context, not from observing my own mechanism, which remains entirely opaque to me.
